(Jehad Alshrafi / Associated Press) By Wafaa Shurafa and Toqa Ezzidin May 7, 2026 9:38 AM PT 3 5 min Click here to listen to this article Hamas says an Israeli strike killed the 32-year-old son of its chief negotiator in Gaza City, with the group accusing Israel of targeting family members to pressure negotiators. Israel’s continued strikes coincide with a U.S.-brokered ceasefire that was supposed to halt major military operations, though critical provisions—including Hamas disarmament and Israeli troop withdrawal—remain unmet. original.title: Here's what we found after Israel's raid on Al-Shifa, Gaza's biggest hospital
original.summary: NPR reports on the Israeli raid of Al-Shifa Hospital in Gaza and the devastating aftermath of the operation.

signals.page_title: Al Shifa hospital: What we found in the wake of Israel's raid : NPR
signals.text_snippet: Accessibility links Skip to main content Keyboard shortcuts for audio player Play Live Radio HOURLY NEWS LISTEN LIVE MY PLAYLIST NEWSLETTERS SIGN IN NPR SHOP DONATE NEWS CULTURE MUSIC PODCASTS & SHOWS SEARCH SPECIAL SERIES Middle East conflict Here's what we found after Israel's raid on Al-Shifa, Gaza's biggest hospital APRIL 6, 20245:02 AM ET By Aya Batrawy , Omar El Qattaa DUBAI and GAZA CITY, Gaza Strip — For two weeks beginning in March, Israeli commandos besieged what was once the Gaza Strip's biggest and most advanced medical complex, Al-Shifa. Israel's military said the target was militants who'd regrouped there, using its premises for shelter and internet access. It said Hamas hid behind the sick and injured, and insisted no civilians were killed in the raid. Israeli statements described it as a precise operation — a model for future military action in Gaza.
